title: The gender wage ratio in different income percentiles
subtitle: The gender ratio refers to the raw female-male wage ratio. "Ninetieth percentile" indicates an income higher than that of 90% of the population - thus, for example, in 2010 a 74% ratio in the ninetieth percentile indicates that the highest earning women made 74% of the hourly wage of the highest earning men.
caption:
The gender wage ratio in different income percentiles, United States, 1980 to 2010
The gender ratio refers to the raw female-male wage ratio. "Ninetieth percentile" indicates an income higher than that of 90% of the population - thus, for example, in 2010 a 74% ratio in the ninetieth percentile indicates that the highest earning women made 74% of the hourly wage of the highest earning men.

Three lines track the raw female-male wage ratio across the United States from 1980 to 2010, measuring earnings for women in the tenth, fiftieth, and ninetieth income percentiles relative to men in the same brackets. The vertical axis spans from 0% to 100%, showing that women consistently earned less than men across all three groups throughout the thirty-year period. The line representing the tenth percentile begins near 65% in 1980 and climbs steadily, reaching just above 80% by 2010, marking the largest visible gain among the three groups. The fiftieth percentile line starts slightly lower, around 60%, and rises to meet the tenth percentile line near the 80% mark by the end of the timeline. The ninetieth percentile line begins near 62% and rises to approximately 75% by 1990, after which it flattens and remains horizontal through 2010. While the lower and middle income groups show continuous upward movement, the highest income group shows a distinct plateau beginning in the early 1990s. The gap between the tenth and ninetieth percentiles narrows significantly over time as the lower-income lines overtake the highest-income line. The data shows that wage ratios improved for lower and middle earners while stagnating for the highest earners in the latter half of the period.
